prompt: Explain what a "closure" is in programming, in exactly one sentence. The sentence can be long.
response: A closure is a programming concept in which a function has access to its own scope, as well as the scope of its outer functions, even when the outer function has returned, allowing the inner function to use and manipulate variables from the outer scope, effectively "closing over" them and preserving their state, even after the outer function is no longer executing.
